data.title: Tether to Shut Down CNH₮ Stablecoin, Sets 2027 Redemption Deadline
data.description:
Tether announced on February 21, 2026, that it is discontinuing support for its offshore Chinese yuan-pegged stablecoin, CNH₮, citing low demand and changing market conditions. The company outlined a structured wind-down process, giving holders one year to redeem their tokens.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/tether-to-shut-down-cnh%e2%82%ae-stablecoin-sets-2027-redemption-deadline/

data.title: Germany's CDU Proposes Social Media Ban for Minors
data.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) party has proposed a ban on the use of social media platforms like Instagram and TikTok for minors in Germany. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the party's concerns about the impact of these platforms on young users. The CDU argues that such a ban would protect minors from potential risks associated with social media use. The proposal has sparked a debate about the balance between online safety and freedom of expression for younger users.

data.title: Tether to discontinue CNH₮ stablecoin, halts new issuance effective immediately
data.description:
Tether has pulled the plug on offshore Chinese yuan-backed stablecoin CNH₮, saying that new tokens will no longer be issued from them anymore. In a press release on Saturday, Tether said, “We continuously evaluate our stablecoin offerings to ensure they align with real-world usage, long-term sustainability, and the needs of the communities that rely on […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/tether-to-discontinue-cnh%e2%82%ae-stablecoin/

data.title: Norwegian Athlete Johannes Hosflot Klaebo Sets Record with Sixth Gold Medal at Games
data.description: Johannes Hosflot Klaebo of Norway achieved a historic milestone on Saturday by winning his sixth gold medal, setting a record for the most golds won by any athlete at a single Games. New York Times posted on X, highlighting Klaebo's remarkable performance and his dominance in the competition. His achievement underscores Norway's strong presence in the Games and Klaebo's exceptional skill and dedication to his sport. The victory adds to his impressive career and solidifies his status as one of the top athletes in the world.

data.title: AI Bot Profits from Micro-Arbitrage in Crypto Markets
data.description: A fully automated AI-driven bot has successfully exploited micro-arbitrage opportunities in short-term cryptocurrency prediction markets, amassing nearly $150,000 in profits. According to NS3.AI, the bot utilized automated trading strategies to repeatedly capture small inefficiencies, capitalizing on minor price discrepancies. This development underscores the increasing influence of AI in retail crypto trading, facilitating more precise and timely market exploitation.

data.title: Severe Winter Storm Threatens New York City and Northeast
data.description: A powerful winter storm is forecasted to impact New York City and the Northeast, potentially developing into a 'super bomb' with significant snowfall starting Sunday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential for heavy, wet snow to accumulate by the foot, posing challenges for residents and authorities in the affected regions. The storm's intensity and trajectory are being closely monitored as preparations are underway to mitigate its impact.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | German Chancellor Merz to Discuss Tariff Dispute with U.S.
data.description: German Chancellor Merz has stated that before taking any action regarding the potential repatriation of billions of dollars by German companies due to U.S. tariff decisions, communication with the U.S. government is necessary. According to Jin10, Chancellor Merz plans to visit Washington in about a week to address the issue.

data.title: Kirkland & Ellis Faces New Rival in Debt Restructuring Arena
data.description: Kirkland & Ellis, a prominent law firm known for handling intricate and lucrative debt restructurings, has encountered a formidable competitor following the exit of a key partner. Bloomberg posted on X that this development marks a significant shift in the competitive landscape of the legal industry, particularly in the realm of financial restructuring. The departure of the star partner has opened the door for other firms to challenge Kirkland & Ellis's dominance in managing complex financial cases. As the restructuring sector continues to evolve, firms are vying for top talent and high-profile cases, reshaping the dynamics of the industry. This change underscores the competitive nature of the legal field, where expertise and reputation play crucial roles in securing major clients and projects.
